response: Alex has always been a firm believer in traditional values, especially when it comes to love and relationships. Recently, they decided to expand their reading habits and explore new ideas. This decision was not made lightly, as it required a significant shift in their usual routine. The first novel Alex read was a challenging read that introduced different perspectives on love. It took Alex four days to finish, which was a considerable amount of time. The novel had 120 pages, making it a relatively short book compared to what was to come. Despite its brevity, it left a lasting impression on Alex. Each day, Alex read a consistent number of pages, which helped them maintain a steady pace. The second novel was more complex, with 180 pages. It took Alex six days to complete, which was longer than the first book. This novel offered deeper insights into the nature of relationships and challenged Alex's previous beliefs. The third novel was the longest of the three, with 240 pages. It took Alex eight days to finish, which was the most time-consuming of all. Each novel required a different amount of time to read, but the number of pages read each day remained constant. This consistency made it easier for Alex to track their progress. The average number of pages read per day was calculated by dividing the total pages by the total number of days. This method ensured that the average was accurate and fair. The first novel contributed 120 pages over four days, resulting in 30 pages per day. The second novel had 180 pages over six days, which also resulted in 30 pages per day. The third novel had 240 pages over eight days, which again gave 30 pages per day. This pattern was consistent across all three novels. The same number of pages was read each day, which made the reading process more predictable. Alex found this consistency helpful in managing their time effectively. Reading the same number of pages each day also helped Alex avoid burnout. The process of reading was more enjoyable when it was structured. Each novel required a different amount of time, but the daily reading goal remained the same. This approach allowed Alex to maintain a steady reading habit. The average number of pages read per day was calculated by adding the total pages and dividing by the total days. This calculation was done for each novel individually. The results were the same for all three novels, which was a surprising yet satisfying discovery. The total number of pages across all three novels was 54
